description: Two households need the same cart, so they negotiate which days each will use it. 'numike' discusses changeable terms with other participants in search of an arrangement they may accept. Negotiation can expose a disagreement, end without a settlement, or produce terms that one participant still refuses.
articulatory_notes: Place the tongue tip against the backs of the upper front teeth for voiced dental 'n' and let the resonance pass through the nose into high-back rounded 'u'. Close the lips for voiced 'm', keep the airflow nasal, and release into stressed high-front unrounded 'i'. Close the back of the tongue against the soft palate for unvoiced 'k' and finish with middle-height front unrounded 'e'.
usage_notes: A negotiating counterpart takes comitative 'nua'. The terms, exchange, boundary, or other matter under negotiation is the direct object. The event-noun rule supplies negotiation. 'shareo' (discuss) keeps a topic before participants without requiring changeable terms, while 'nawo' (agree) may name an outcome rather than the process that sought it. Module word 'manawi' (compromise) names an arrangement in which participants each yield something they value.

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Negotiation keeps terms open to speech and revision while every participant retains the ability to refuse. A settlement reached through pressure still needs the pressure named.

semantic_domains.activity: working through changeable terms towards a possible arrangement
semantic_domains.communication: discussing terms with one or more counterparts
semantic_domains.community: seeking an arrangement among participants with differing positions
